paragraphs: Across the two statements, the biggest cumulative cost issue is the processor-controlled access fee, which totaled $119.79 over the review period and warrants pass-through documentation because it was charged at the same rate across independent networks. If the processor cannot document the wholesale basis, this is one of the clearest opportunities to challenge the charge. The statements also show a hidden processor-controlled monthly advantage markup of $29.71 and a per-authorization fee above benchmark totaling $70.66, both of which add avoidable cost beyond true card-network pass-through items.

Two fees appeared without prior notice in the analyzed period and should be treated as especially important to validate. MANAGED SECURITY NON VALIDATED first appeared on both statements and totaled $99.90; the explanation says PCI compliance validation has not been completed, so completing the PCI Self-Assessment Questionnaire or correcting the terminal/gateway setup should eliminate or significantly reduce it. BENTOBOX ONLINE ORDER FEE also appeared on both statements and totaled $34.65; this is a third-party online-ordering service charge, so it should be verified as active and needed before it is kept or renegotiated.

The data also shows several rate issues that deserve different handling depending on whether they are processor-controlled or pass-through. The per-authorization fee is above the competitive benchmark and the monthly advantage fee adds hidden percentage markup, so those items are best handled through negotiation. By contrast, ACCESS FEE, DISCOVER AUTH FEE, and MC LICENSE VOLUME FEE were all identified as charges that should be supported by pass-through documentation; if that support is missing, the charges should be disputed. Other minor rate exceedances were also captured in the totals, even though they were not shown individually.

The effective rate trend was stable, moving from 2.50% in 2024-11 to 2.48% in 2024-12, with the overall average at 2.49%. That stability suggests the cost picture was driven by both volume and fee mix rather than a single isolated spike. Total volume across the review period was $109,634.41 and total fees were $2,726.05, so the main improvement path is fee reduction rather than relying on volume shifts alone. The benchmark status is within the competitive range for a Restaurant / Food Service account at this volume, which means the focus should stay on targeted optimization opportunities rather than a wholesale pricing reset.

Prioritized action items, in the order provided, are: Priority 1, complete PCI/security validation or correct the terminal/gateway setup, with expected annual savings of $604.80 and no negotiation required; Priority 2, ask the processor for pass-through documentation and dispute the charge if it cannot be documented, with expected annual savings of $780.21 and investigation required; Priority 3, verify the third-party service is active and cancel or renegotiate it if it is not needed, with expected annual savings of $207.90 and investigation required; Priority 4, negotiate the processor-controlled rate, with expected annual savings of $602.22 and negotiation required; Priority 5, ask whether batching can be consolidated or the per-batch settlement fee can be waived, with expected annual savings of $106.20 and negotiation required.

Total projected annual savings if all actions are taken is $2,301.33.

benchmark.message: within competitive range (1.95% - 2.45% for Restaurant / Food Service at this volume)

topFindings.title: MANAGED SECURITY NON VALIDATED may be avoidable through configuration or qualification fixes
topFindings.explanation: PCI compliance validation has not been completed. Complete the PCI Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ). Once validated, this fee is eliminated or significantly reduced.
topFindings.action: Complete PCI/security validation or correct the terminal/gateway setup.

evidence.detail: MANAGED SECURITY NON VALIDATED may be avoidable through configuration or qualification fixes: monthly impact 49.95, projected annual impact 599.40.
MANAGED SECURITY NON VALIDATED may be avoidable through configuration or qualification fixes: monthly impact 49.95, projected annual impact 599.40.

topFindings.title: ACCESS FEE is charged at the same rate across independent networks
topFindings.explanation: This pattern suggests a processor-controlled access fee may be applied uniformly across independent networks. Ask for pass-through documentation showing the source and exact wholesale basis.
topFindings.action: Ask the processor for pass-through documentation and dispute the charge if it cannot be documented.

evidence.detail: ACCESS FEE is charged at the same rate across independent networks: monthly impact 58.79, projected annual impact 705.48.
ACCESS FEE is charged at the same rate across independent networks: monthly impact 61.00, projected annual impact 732.00.

topFindings.title: BENTOBOX ONLINE ORDER FEE is a third-party service fee
topFindings.explanation: This appears to be a third-party online-ordering service charge. Verify that the service is active, wanted, and priced correctly.
topFindings.action: Verify the third-party service is active and cancel or renegotiate it if it is not needed.

evidence.detail: BENTOBOX ONLINE ORDER FEE 17 TRANSACTIONS AT 0.99 is a third-party service fee: monthly impact 16.83, projected annual impact 201.96.
BENTOBOX ONLINE ORDER FEE 18 TRANSACTIONS AT 0.99 is a third-party service fee: monthly impact 17.82, projected annual impact 213.84.

topFindings.title: DISCOVER AUTH FEE exceeds the reference rate
topFindings.explanation: This is a per-authorization fee charged by the processor. It is applied to every card transaction and is negotiable. If multiple per-auth fees are stacked, the total per-authorization cost may be higher than competitive rates.
topFindings.action: Ask the processor for pass-through documentation and dispute the charge if it cannot be documented.

evidence.detail: DISCOVER AUTH FEE 28 TRANSACTIONS AT 0.1 exceeds the reference rate: monthly impact 2.73, projected annual impact 32.76.
DISCOVER AUTH FEE 29 TRANSACTIONS AT 0.1 exceeds the reference rate: monthly impact 2.83, projected annual impact 33.96.

topFindings.title: MC LICENSE VOLUME FEE exceeds the reference rate
topFindings.explanation: This is a Mastercard acquirer license fee assessed as a percentage of volume. It is a network pass-through fee, but the rate varies by acquirer and is not publicly fixed at a single rate. If the rate exceeds commonly cited industry references, request documentation from your processor.
topFindings.action: Ask the processor for pass-through documentation and dispute the charge if it cannot be documented.

evidence.detail: MC LICENSE VOLUME FEE 0.000217 DISC RATE TIMES $12970.74 exceeds the reference rate: monthly impact 1.84, projected annual impact 22.08.
MC LICENSE VOLUME FEE 0.000217 DISC RATE TIMES $12979.96 exceeds the reference rate: monthly impact 1.85, projected annual impact 22.20.

topFindings.title: Per-authorization fee is above competitive benchmark
topFindings.explanation: This is processor-controlled per-authorization pricing. The finding measures potential savings from reducing the per-auth cost to the competitive benchmark target.

evidence.detail: Per-authorization fee is above competitive benchmark: monthly impact 34.66, projected annual impact 415.92.
Per-authorization fee is above competitive benchmark: monthly impact 36.00, projected annual impact 432.00.

topFindings.title: MONTHLY ADVANTAGE FEE MCVDB adds hidden percentage markup
topFindings.explanation: This appears to be a processor-controlled percentage markup embedded as a monthly advantage fee. It is negotiable and should be separated from true card-network pass-through costs.

evidence.detail: MONTHLY ADVANTAGE FEE MCVDB 0.0003 TIMES $47996.83 adds hidden percentage markup: monthly impact 14.40, projected annual impact 172.80.
MONTHLY ADVANTAGE FEE MCVDB 0.0003 TIMES $51022.27 adds hidden percentage markup: monthly impact 15.31, projected annual impact 183.72.
